title: Add conformance testing to CI
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

> /kind cleanup

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Moves the conformance tests from ginkgo to using the default testing package and github.com/stretchr/testify/require, preserving the existing tests and adding more.

Add conformance tests to our Cirrus configuration, currently marked as an allowed failure while we get known failures fixed.

#### How to verify it

Running conformance tests as described in tests/conformance/README.md should still flag some known differences between what we produce and what `docker build` produces with dockerd 19.03.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

This should help spot errors in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2480 and ensure that it doesn't introduce regressions.  Some failures will be fixed by https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/170.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
None
```

comments.body: @nalind looks like lint isn't buying today
Oh, to be clear, many of these tests don't pass.  We're going to fix that.
